key_points: Human resources refer to people and their skills, knowledge, and abilities
Population distribution refers to the way people are spread across the Earth
Population density is the number of people living per unit area of land
Population distribution is influenced by physical and human factors
Human resource development focuses on improving people's skills, education, and health
examples: Densely populated regions: South Asia and East Asia
Sparsely populated regions: Polar regions and deserts
Physical factor example: Availability of water and favorable climate
Human factor example: Job opportunities in cities
Migration example: Movement of people from rural areas to urban areas
important_terms: Human Resources: People and their skills that contribute to economic and social development
Population Distribution: The pattern of where people live on Earth
Population Density: Number of people living per unit area
Migration: Movement of people from one place to another
Human Resource Development: Improving education, skills, and health of people
exam_tips: Understand the meaning of human resources and their importance
Remember the formula for calculating population density
Learn the physical and human factors affecting population distribution
Understand the concepts of push and pull factors in migration
Study how education and healthcare improve human resource development
